title: Upon The Sight Of A Beautiful Picture Painted By Sir G. H. Beaumont, Bart
content:
Praised be the Art whose subtle power could stay
Yon cloud, and fix it in that glorious shape;
Nor would permit the thin smoke to escape,
Nor those bright sunbeams to forsake the day;
Which stopped that band of travellers on their way,
Ere they were lost within the shady wood;
And showed the Bark upon the glassy flood
For ever anchored in her sheltering bay.
Soul-soothing Art! whom Morning, Noontide, Even,
Do serve with all their changeful pageantry;
Thou, with ambition modest yet sublime,
Here, for the sight of mortal man, hast given
To one brief moment caught from fleeting time
The appropriate calm of blest eternity.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Wordsworth delivers a powerful performance in "Upon The Sight Of A Beautiful Picture Painted By Sir G. H. Beaumont, Bart"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
